title: rosa scabrata var. typica
intro: Rosa scabrata var. typica, also known as the rough-leaved rose, is a type of wild rose that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Rosa scabrata var. typica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and soothe skin irritations. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being.
